evals.prompt: I have a Next.js SaaS project and want to set up QA project context from scratch
expected_patterns: Product section with name and URLs
Risk Areas table with at least 3 entries
anti_patterns: Risk Areas section empty or missing

evals.prompt: Set up QA context for my project. I have Playwright and Jest already, running on GitHub Actions
expected_patterns: Test Stack section populated with Playwright and Jest
anti_patterns: asking about Playwright when playwright.config.ts exists
generic CI/CD section without GitHub Actions specifics

evals.prompt: My .agents/qa-project-context.md already exists but has some [bracketed placeholders]. Update only the empty sections
expected_patterns: list which sections are complete vs. unfilled
ask only about the unfilled sections

evals.prompt: I need to identify critical risk areas for my e-commerce platform. What questions should you ask to fill the Risk Areas section?
expected_patterns: Which parts cause the most production incidents
Area | Risk Level | Business Impact | Notes
anti_patterns: Risk table with fewer than 3 entries

evals.prompt: I'm a solo developer with no QA team. How should the Team section be filled for my context?
My project is a monorepo with 3 separate frontend apps and a shared API. How should I structure the Tech Stack and Test Stack sections?
expected_patterns: Shard E2E per app OR per-site subdirectories
shards independently OR path filters gate which app
anti_patterns: flat Tech Stack without distinguishing components
unclear which tests run for which app

evals.prompt: My team has Playwright E2E tests but no unit tests yet. What should the Test Stack section say, and which skill should I use next?
expected_patterns: Vitest recommended OR Vitest (unit) as defaults
anti_patterns: leaving 'TBD' or [unit framework] in Test Stack
no guidance on what to do next

evals.prompt: Set up quality goals section for my early-stage startup. What realistic coverage and speed targets should I include?
anti_patterns: aspirational goals like 'we want great quality'

evals.prompt: How do I fill the Conventions section for selector strategy if my team uses semantic selectors (ARIA roles)?
When I say my context file is done, what validations should confirm it's complete and ready for other skills to use?
expected_patterns: Product, Tech Stack, Test Stack, CI/CD, Environments, Quality
Risk Areas table has at least 3 entries
at least 5 specific, testable key user flows
actual headcount and the dev:QA ratio (or "solo")
Quality Goals are concrete numbers (coverage %, flake %, durations)
anti_patterns: vague user flows like 'user uses app'
